The current completion rate for Toolbox refresher training, standing at 94.44%, raises significant concern as
it demonstrates a clear shortfall against the required 100% compliance standard over the past 12 months.
This gap in training completion increases the risk that staff may not be fully up to date with essential safety
protocols, potentially impacting overall operational safety. Immediate action is needed to address the low
participation rate and ensure full adherence to mandatory training requirements.

Photo 16
Contractor & Visitor Safety
Are contractors managed whilst working on site? (sign
in/out, permit to work) On arrival, I wasn’t asked to sign in, leaving no official record of my presence on site. This lack of registration
means there would be no immediate way to account for my whereabouts in the event of an emergency.
Similarly, without signing out, there is no reliable record of when I left, increasing the risk of inaccurate site
occupancy records.
No
Manual Handling
Is manual handling being carried out safely and are good
practices being followed and posters visible? During the inspection, no clearly visible manual handling poster could be located in the designated area,
which could hinder staff awareness of safe practices. It was eventually found concealed beneath a stack of
boxes, suggesting that routine checks and accessibility measures may not be consistently maintained.
No
COSHH
COSHH data sheets available on site? The absence of COSHH safety data sheets during the inspection raises significant concerns regarding the
management and communication of hazardous substances on site. Without accessible documentation, staff
may not have the necessary information to handle materials safely, increasing the risk of improper storage,
usage, or emergency response. Immediate action is recommended to ensure all relevant safety data sheets
are readily available and up to date.
No
Working at Height
Are all ladders clearly numbered for identification purposes? During the inspection, it was observed that all ladders were clearly numbered in accordance with
requirements, yet several step stools lacked any identification. The absence of numbering makes it difficult
to track their condition or include them in routine checks, increasing the likelihood that defects could go
unnoticed. This oversight presents a preventable safety risk and should be addressed swiftly to maintain
proper control and accountability of all access equipment.
No
Fire Safety
Structure found to be in a good condition with no evidence
of damage which would compromise fire safety - EG Missing
c wiling tiles / gaps from area to area?
In the stock and staff room, there were a couple of missing ceiling tiles. The building’s structural integrity
and internal compartmentation present as sound, with surfaces and barriers well maintained. There are no
other evident breaches or gaps that could facilitate the movement of fire or smoke, indicating effective
containment. Overall, the current condition supports the intended level of fire safety within the premises.
No
Disclaimer
Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

Yes
Are adequate facilities provided to rest and eat meals? Staff welfare provision includes a suitable break area that is kept clean, comfortable, and easily accessible to

Yes

ensure the door retains its fire resisting properties and
holds back the blaze to enable persons to escape?
Fire doors are fitted with intumescent strips which expand when exposed to high temperatures, sealing

maintaining the integrity of emergency egress and limiting potential damage.
Yes

fra_custom_data.occupancy: To be calculated based on floor area
fra_custom_data.description: The premises, located in Crown Point North Shopping Park, consists of two floors and is primarily used for retail operations. It employs 17 staff members, with a maximum of 5 on site at any one time.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.adjacentOccupancies: The Footasylum store in Denton is a terraced retail unit physically adjoined to other shops within a single, continuous building block at Crown Point Shopping Park. It is directly sandwiched between River Island and Clarks, sharing structural walls and a common roofline with both. This building segment also includes other major retailers like New Look and JD Sports, which are part of the same interconnected terrace. While each shop has its own independent storefront and entrance, they are structurally part of the same long-form building designed to house multiple commercial tenants under one footprint.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises is located at Unit 8B Broughton Shopping Park Chester Road, Broughton and is arranged over 2 level(s) (Ground Floor and First Floor), comprising:
• Main sales floor to the front of the unit
• Stockroom, staff welfare facilities and management office to the rear
• Rear service corridor providing access to fire exits
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.adjacentOccupancies: Nearby retail occupancies include Asda Living, Primark, SportsDirect, JD Sports.

Site conducted Br 162 Leeds White Rose, North,
NORTH EAST - Genen Higgs

Location
Leeds
England
LS11
United Kingdom
(53.759247575735415,

-- 1 of 66 --

1.Flaggeditems
Flagged items 8 flagged
Training
H&S induction training onboarding up to date and at 100%? No
At the time of inspection, the incomplete onboarding of three staff members raises concerns about their
familiarity with essential workplace procedures and protocols. This gap could increase the likelihood of
errors or unsafe practices, as they may not yet be fully aware of all operational and compliance
requirements.
Photo 14
Training
H&S toolbox refresher training completed in the last 12
months and records available for
Manual handling
Housekeeping
Fire Safety
Stepladders
No
The current completion rate for Toolbox refresher training is concerning, with only 77.02% of staff having
complied over the past 12 months. This shortfall against the 100% target suggests a significant gap in
ensuring that all team members remain up to date with essential safety knowledge. Such a lapse could
present heightened risks in day-to-day operations if not promptly addressed.
Photo 15
Contractor & Visitor Safety
Are contractors managed whilst working on site? (sign
in/out, permit to work) No
I wasn’t asked to sign in or out on my arrival, which meant my presence went unacknowledged. This lack of
oversight could create confusion in the event of an emergency, as there would be no reliable record of who
was on site.
COSHH
Are chemicals stored correctly? No
The COSHH cupboard is adequately stocked with all necessary materials and is conveniently situated in the
staff break area, ensuring easy access for employees. However, the presence of a broken cupboard door
poses a potential risk, as it could compromise safe storage and allow accidental exposure to hazardous
substances.

-- 2 of 66 --

Photo 40 Photo 41
COSHH
COSHH data sheets available on site? No
The absence of COSHH safety data sheets during the inspection presents a significant gap in hazard
communication and control measures. Without immediate access to these documents, staff cannot verify
safe handling procedures or confirm the correct response to potential chemical exposure incidents. This
omission indicates a need for stronger on-site documentation management to ensure readiness and
compliance.
Working at Height
Are all ladders clearly numbered for identification purposes? No
During the inspection, it was observed that several ladders and steps lacked clear numbering, which could
create confusion during routine operations or emergency situations. This absence of identification increases
the likelihood of miscommunication when accessing elevated areas and may hinder efficient incident
reporting or maintenance checks.
Photo 85 Photo 86
Fire Safety
Fire doors in a good condition? No
The fire door previously noted remains non-compliant, with a visible gap that the intumescent strip fails to
seal. This presents a clear weakness in the door’s ability to contain smoke and flames in the event of a fire.
Immediate remedial action is necessary to ensure the integrity of the fire protection measures.
Photo 99
Fire Safety
Are fire door intumescent strips in place and intact, to
ensure the door retains its fire resisting properties and
holds back the blaze to enable persons to escape?
No
The presence of intumescent strips on older doors provides essential protection by slowing the spread of
heat and smoke in the event of a fire, significantly improving overall safety. In contrast, the absence of these
strips on the new doors represents a reduction in passive fire protection and could compromise the integrity
of the building’s fire safety measures.

-- 3 of 66 --

Photo 100 Photo 101 Photo 102 Photo 103 Photo 104 Photo 105

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises operates over one level (Ground Floor) and comprises a main sales floor to the front of the unit with associated back-of-house areas to the rear, including stockroom, office and staff welfare facilities.
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
0 / 0(0%)
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises is located at Unit 47 Liverpool Shopping Park Edge Lane, Liverpool and operates over one level (Ground Floor) with a main sales floor to the front of the unit and associated back-of-house areas to the rear, including stockroom, office and staff welfare facilities.
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.

fra_custom_data.occupancy:
Standard (60 sq ft/person): 6,400 sq ft ÷ 60 = ~107 people
Peak (30 sq ft/person): 6,400 sq ft ÷ 30 = ~213 people
fra_custom_data.description:
The premises is located at Unit 2B Parkgate Shopping Park Stadium Way, Rotherham and operates over one level (Ground Floor) with a main sales floor to the front of the unit and associated back-of-house areas to the rear, including stockroom, office and staff welfare facilities.
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.operatingHours: Monday to Friday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Sunday: 10:30 AM – 4:30 PM

fra_extracted_data.fireDrillDate: A fire drill is documented as being conducted in the last 6 months, but no date has been recorded.
fra_extracted_data.operatingHours: Monday to Friday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Sunday: 10:30 AM – 4:30 PM
fra_extracted_data.firePanelFaults: The fire panel was free of faults at the time of inspection
fra_extracted_data.weeklyFireTests: Weekly tests carried out on a Sunday and recorded on Zipline on the weekly checks.
fra_extracted_data.fireDoorsCondition: All fire doors closed and in good working order and feature intumescent strips which ensure the fire doors retain fire resisting properties.
fra_extracted_data.escapeRoutesEvidence: At the time of inspection, all exit routes were clear and unobstructed.
fra_extracted_data.compartmentationStatus: Structure found to be in a good condition with no evidence of damage which would compromise fire safety.
fra_extracted_data.fireSafetyTrainingNarrative: At the time of inspection H&S Toolbox refresher training sat at 77.27% which falls short of the 100% required
fra_extracted_data.emergencyLightingMonthlyTest: Monthly tests carried out and recorded on Zipline on the weekly checks. Last tested 4/01/2026

open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the

to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises is located at 26 Norfolk Street, Baltic Triangle, Liverpool and is arranged over 2 level(s) (Ground Floor and First Floor), comprising:
• Main sales floor to the front of the unit
• Stockroom, staff welfare facilities and management office to the rear
• Rear service corridor providing access to fire exits
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.adjacentOccupancies: Nearby retail occupancies include Baltic View residential building, The Vault under construction, Baltic Creative accommodation.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises operates over one level (Ground Floor) and comprises a main sales floor to the front of the unit with associated back-of-house areas to the rear, including stockroom, office and staff welfare facilities.
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises is located at Unit D11 Eagles Meadow Shopping Centre Smithfield Rd, Wrexham and is arranged over 2 level(s) (Ground Floor and First Floor), comprising:
• Main sales floor to the front of the unit
• Stockroom, staff welfare facilities and management office to the rear
• Rear service corridor providing access to fire exits
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.operatingHours: Monday to Saturday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sunday: 10:30 AM - 4:30 PM

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

fra_custom_data.description:
The premises is located at Unit 33 St Stephens Centre, Hull and operates over one level (Ground Floor) with a main sales floor to the front of the unit and associated back-of-house areas to the rear, including stockroom, office and staff welfare facilities.
The unit is of modern construction, consisting primarily of steel frame with blockwork, modern internal wall finishes and commercial-grade floor coverings.
The premises is a mid-unit with adjoining retail occupancies to either side.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.adjacentOccupancies: Nearby retail occupancies include Superdry, Next, Boots, Flannels.

fra_extracted_data.firePanelFaults: The fire panel indicates no faults of the time of inspection

fra_extracted_data.fireDoorsCondition: Some fire doors were identified as to not having intumescent strips present missing
fra_extracted_data.escapeRoutesEvidence: Escape routeway checked and we’re clear of obstruction at the time of inspection supporting effective evacuation
fra_extracted_data.callPointAccessibility: Manual call points were unobstructed and accessible
fra_extracted_data.compartmentationStatus: The building structure and internal separation appears in good condition with no obvious damage missing ceiling tiles or gaps that observed that would likely compromise fire safety or allow fire/smoke spread between areas Yes
fra_extracted_data.emergencyLightingSwitch: Rear fire door by alarm panel
fra_extracted_data.fireExtinguisherService: Escalators - Service and Maintenance? Last conducted 04/11/2025
fra_extracted_data.fireSafetyTrainingNarrative: All staff have completed Toolbox refresh it training completion is currently below to require standard. The stores compliant figure shows 77.29% which fall short of the target to 100% for the last 12 months.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

fra_custom_data.description: The premises is a single-floor retail space located at 27-29 Corporation Street, Bolton. It features a shop floor and stockrooms.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.
fra_custom_data.adjacentOccupancies: Nearby retail occupancies include Zara, H&M, Topshop, Next.

fra_extracted_data.firePanelFaults: The fire panel indicates no faults of the time of inspection
fra_extracted_data.firePanelLocation: Rear fire door and elec cupboard
fra_extracted_data.fireDoorsCondition: All fire doors were in good condition at the time of inspection, with no signs of damage, warping, or obstruction to their operation. Each door was observed to close securely and form an effective seal, ensuring they would function as intended in the event of a fire.
fra_extracted_data.escapeRoutesEvidence: Several fire escape routes were obstructed by combustibles, stored racks, and rails, creating potential hazards during an emergency. In some areas, fire doors had items placed behind them, preventing them from opening fully and compromising safe evacuation. Immediate removal of these obstacles is necessary to restore clear and compliant escape routes.
fra_extracted_data.compartmentationStatus: hazards during an emergency. In some areas, fire doors had items placed behind them, preventing them from opening fully and compromising safe evacuation. Immediate removal of these obstacles is necessary to restore clear and compliant escape routes.
fra_extracted_data.managementReviewStatement: This assessment has been informed by recent health and safety inspections and site observations.
fra_extracted_data.fireSafetyTrainingNarrative: All staff have now successfully completed their onboarding, ensuring they are fully briefed on workplace procedures and operational requirements. This thorough induction means everyone is aware of the correct protocols, reducing the likelihood of errors and improving overall site safety. The team is well prepared to carry out their duties confidently and responsibly from day one. The current completion rate for Toolbox refresher training, standing at 55.56%, raises significant concern as it demonstrates a clear shortfall against the required 100% compliance standard over the past 12 months. This gap in training completion increases the risk that staff may not be fully up to date with essential safety protocols, potentially impacting overall operational safety. Immediate action is needed to address the low participation rate and ensure full adherence to mandatory training requirements.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
When hazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NW Ltd limits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NW Ltd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.

fra_custom_data.description: Our Liverpool One store is ideally located within the Liverpool One Shopping Centre on Paradise Street. Specialising in the latest branded footwear, accessories and apparel for men, women and children, Footasylum has something for everyone. We have a huge collection of the biggest brands to choose from, including Monterrain, Nike, Montirex, Under Armour, The North Face, Berghaus and more. Whether you’re looking for the freshest pair of trainers, or to shop the latest trends in our men's, women's and kids' collections, you’re sure to find something at the Footasylum Liverpool One store. The Footasylum Liverpool One store is easily accessible by bus, train, bike or car, with city centre car parking near to Liverpool One.
fra_custom_data.propertyType: Retail unit used for the sale of branded fashion apparel and footwear to members of the public.

fra_extracted_data.firePanelFaults: At the time of the inspection, the fire panel was observed to be fully operational, with no active faults or warnings displayed. All indicator lights were functioning correctly, and the system appeared to be monitoring the premises as intended. Functional checks confirmed that there were no signs of system errors, unusual noises, or performance irregularities. This suggests that the fire detection system is currently in good working order and ready to respond in the event of an emergency. Routine monitoring and scheduled maintenance should continue to ensure ongoing reliability and compliance with operational standards.
fra_extracted_data.weeklyFireTests: Conducted and documented on the weekly store checks
fra_extracted_data.fireDoorsCondition:
Good condition; intumescent strips present; Some doors in the stock room areas are propped open at the time of inspection, reducing the intended
separation between spaces. This creates a potential pathway for smoke and fire to travel, undermining the
protection these barriers are designed to provide. Immediate attention to closing and securing these doors
would help maintain the safety of the premises.
fra_extracted_data.escapeRoutesEvidence: Observed during recent inspections: fire exits and delivery doors were partially blocked by pallets and boxes (stockroom and rear fire door), restricting effective escape width during evacuation.
fra_extracted_data.managementReviewStatement: This assessment has been informed by recent health and safety inspections and site observations.
fra_extracted_data.fireSafetyTrainingNarrative: Fire safety training is delivered via induction and toolbox talks; H&S Induction Training / Onboarding up to date at 100%.
fra_extracted_data.emergencyLightingMonthlyTest: Conducted and documented on the weekly store checks

Health and Safety Assessment Terms and Conditions:
It is agreed that to enable a thorough inspection and assessment, the Assessor will be allowed
open and free access to any sites and any areas within those sites covered by the contract.
It is the responsibility of the Customer to ensure that all relevant personnel are fully aware of the
Assessors visit and that the Assessor or others working on behalf of the Assessing Company are
not hindered in any way in the carrying out of their duties
The Assessor will use all best endeavours to advise the Customer as to the status, regulations and
systems of work appropriate to the Customer’s fire risk assessment so as to assist the Customer
to fulfil their duties in accordance with such statutes, regulations, standards and such systems of
work which may be applicable to the Customers specific industry.
The Assessment will be based on a sample of the working environment as identified at the time
of the Assessors visit. It is possible that this sample may not be entirely representative of all
aspects of the working environment.
Whilst the Assessor will use best endeavours to identify those matters which are considered
unacceptable to current fire safety regulations and which have been observed during the
inspection, KSS NW Ltd cannot be held responsible for non-compliance with relevant statutes,
regulations and standards or systems of work or for failing to observe industry fire and safety
working practices whether such non-compliance or failure is by the Customer, it’s servants,
agents, contractors or sub-contractors.
The Assessor should be notified of any visit or intended visit to be made to the premises by an
Enforcement Authority or Insurance assessor prior to carrying out the assessment.
The Assessment will be based on a visual inspection or the working environment, no testing of
the luminance levels or sounders, of the design or the HVAC system will be carried out.
If requirements or recommendations are made by an Enforcement Authority, Insurance Risk
Assessor or KSS NW Ltd, it is recommended that Customer carry out these requirements or
recommendations as soon as is reasonably practicable. Notification of completion of relevant
parts should be made to KSS NW Ltd in writing.
The Assessor must be immediately notified at the time of the visit of any recommendation or
requirements made by the Enforcement Authority or Insurance Risk Assessor. It is the
Customer’s responsibility to comply with such recommendations or requirements.
Whenhazards are identified which in the opinion of the Assessor requires the advice of a
particular specialist, KSS NW Ltd will notify the Customer accordingly and will indicate where in its
opinion such advice and service may be obtained. It shall be the Customer’s decision whether or
not to use the particular specialist. The cost of such specialist advice shall at all times remain the
responsibility of the Customer.
KSS NWLtdlimits its liability for any loss, damage or injury (or consequential or indirect loss)
arising form the performance of, or failure by, KSS NW Ltd to perform any of its duties (whether
or not such loss damage or injury or consequential or indirect loss be due to the negligence of
KSS NWLtd, its servants or agents or to any other cause whatsoever) to that determined by our
Insurance Policy.
